title: Team Lead (Life Cycle Electornics Company)
description:
Job Description

1. Team Leadership & Supervision

  • Lead, supervise, and motivate an operations team handling lifecycle services such as records management, data storage, asset tracking, and secure destruction.

  • Plan and allocate daily manpower to meet operational schedules, SLAs, and productivity targets.

  • Conduct daily briefings, toolbox meetings, and performance check-ins with team members.

  • Provide on-the-job training, coaching, and guidance to ensure team competency and compliance.

2. Operations & Workflow Management

  • Oversee end-to-end operational activities including receiving, cataloguing, storage, retrieval, transportation, and disposal of physical and digital assets.

  • Ensure all processes follow Iron Mountain’s standard operating procedures (SOPs), quality standards, and service level agreements.

  • Monitor daily operations to ensure accuracy, timeliness, and minimal errors.

  • Escalate and resolve operational issues, delays, or exceptions promptly.

3. Safety, Security & Compliance

  • Ensure strict adherence to safety protocols, security controls, and regulatory requirements (e.g. data protection, chain of custody).

  • Conduct regular safety checks and ensure proper use of equipment, tools, and PPE.

  • Enforce confidentiality and information security standards at all times.

  • Support audits, inspections, and compliance reporting as required.

4. Inventory & Asset Control

  • Oversee inventory accuracy through proper tracking, labeling, and system updates.

  • Ensure all asset movements are correctly recorded in warehouse management or lifecycle systems.

  • Investigate discrepancies, losses, or damages and implement corrective actions.

5. Continuous Improvement & Reporting

  • Track team performance metrics such as productivity, accuracy, turnaround time, and incidents.

  • Prepare daily or weekly operational reports for management.

  • Identify process improvement opportunities to enhance efficiency, quality, and cost control.

  • Support implementation of new systems, workflows, or operational initiatives.

6. Stakeholder Coordination

  • Coordinate closely with internal departments such as planning, transport, customer service, and compliance teams.

  • Support customer requests and ensure service expectations are met or exceeded.

  • Act as the first point of escalation for operational or service-related issues.

Requirements / Qualifications

  • Diploma or equivalent qualification in Logistics, Operations, Supply Chain, or related field.

  • Minimum 2–4 years of operations experience, with at least 1–2 years in a supervisory or team lead role.

  • Experience in warehouse operations, records management, logistics, or lifecycle services is an advantage.

  • Strong leadership, communication, and people management skills.

  • Familiar with SOP-driven environments and safety-conscious operations.

  • Comfortable using operational systems (WMS, inventory or asset tracking systems).

  • Able to work in a fast-paced, operational environment and handle multiple priorities.
